companies.name: AMD
companies.description: Advanced Micro Devices; AI chips, CPUs, GPUs, and data-center accelerators.
companies.reason: Latest coverage highlights renewed AI-chip momentum, customer wins, and continued investor focus on its data-center/AI roadmap. Reuters and market commentary point to AMD as a key beneficiary of the ongoing AI infrastructure trade.

companies.name: Micron Technology
companies.description: Memory and storage semiconductor maker; key supplier of HBM and DRAM for AI servers.
companies.reason: Strong AI-memory demand, upbeat forecasts, and reports of major customer commitments are keeping Micron in the news. Reuters coverage also notes its huge market-cap rally tied to AI memory shortages and pricing power.

companies.name: Intel
companies.description: Legacy chipmaker and foundry challenger; CPUs, GPUs, and contract manufacturing.
companies.reason: Intel is getting fresh attention from reported large AI-chip manufacturing orders and renewed foundry interest, including Google/Alphabet-linked chip production news. That makes it a major turnaround semiconductor story right now.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.description: Cloud, enterprise software, and AI platform leader; Azure is one of the top hyperscale cloud providers.
companies.reason: Reuters reported Microsoft launching a new company to help enterprises adopt AI technologies, while broader July coverage continues to focus on Azure growth, record capex, and AI monetization.

companies.name: SK Hynix
companies.description: Major memory chipmaker and one of the leading suppliers of HBM for AI servers.
companies.reason: Very fresh news around its planned Nasdaq ADR listing and expansion to capitalize on AI-driven memory demand. This is one of the clearest newly trending semiconductor stories in the latest news flow.
